condition:
Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly forms a stylized quadruped in a leaping pose, oriented along a roughly diagonal axis. The overall bounding box spans approximately 260.2 × 128.7 × 114.9 mm with an aspect ratio of about 2.27, and the layout is tagged as a linear chain along a world-space axis of roughly [−0.70, −0.70, 0.14], consistent with a head-to-tail progression. The hierarchy is flat (tree depth 1) with 4 instances drawn from 4 unique part classes, 2 of which are grounded.

The largest two parts — 'figurine_body' (~110.9 × 114.9 × 117.5 mm, ~332,171 mm³ volume, 109 B-spline faces) and 'ornamental_figurine' (~110.9 × 114.9 × 232.6 mm, ~340,406 mm³ volume, 111 B-spline faces) — together constitute the torso, limbs, and tail. Each participates in 5 freeform contact pairs. The compact 'sculptural_head_shell' (~40.3 × 43.7 × 57.6 mm, ~29,104 mm³, 40 B-spline faces) provides the head with pointed ears, eye indentations, and a small cylindrical nose detail (radius ~2.30 mm, axial extent ~2.06 mm); it participates in all 11 freeform contacts, making it the assembly's central mating hub. A fourth part has no extracted geometry data but contributes one contact. All 11 assembly contacts are freeform (B-spline-to-B-spline); there are no fastener holes, cylindrical fits, or planar mating interfaces recorded. The surface-type histogram across the entire assembly confirms 261 B-spline faces, 10 planar faces, 2 cylindrical faces, and 1 elliptical-cylinder face, underscoring the overwhelmingly sculptural character.

Parts:
- figurine_body: A near-cubic freeform solid (bbox ~111 × 115 × 118 mm, aspect ratio 1.06) shaped as an organic quadruped-like sculptural form, composed almost entirely of B-spline surfaces (109 of 112 faces) with only three small planar faces sharing a downward-pointing normal that likely represents a flat base. It has no holes, no cylindrical features, and interfaces with neighbouring parts exclusively through 5 Nurbs-on-Nurbs curved surface contacts. The part appears to serve as a sculptural component in a multi-piece freeform assembly, with the flat base potentially permitting stable seating on a planar surface.
- ornamental_figurine: A freeform solid depicting a leaping quadruped animal, measuring roughly 111 × 115 × 233 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 2.10). Its geometry is almost entirely B-spline surfaces (111 of 115 faces) with no holes, no cylindrical features, and only 4 minor planar faces. The part participates in 5 Nurbs-on-Nurbs assembly contacts, suggesting it interfaces with neighbouring components through freeform surface mating. It appears to serve as an ornamental or emblematic sculptural element that likely attaches via surface-contact nesting rather than through conventional fastener connections.
- part: 
- sculptural_head_shell: A compact, predominantly freeform solid (bbox ≈ 40 × 44 × 58 mm, aspect ratio 1.43) shaped like a stylized animal head with pointed ears, a tapered snout, and subtle eye recesses; 40 of its 43 faces are B-spline surfaces. A single small cylindrical feature (r ≈ 2.30 mm, 2.06 mm deep) appears at the snout tip, and two planar faces share the normal [1, 0, 0], likely forming a flat mating plane. The part engages 11 Nurbs-on-Nurbs contact pairs, suggesting it nests with multiple freeform neighbouring bodies along curved seams. It appears to serve as the primary outer head shell in a segmented sculptural assembly.

Annotation Caveats:
Part 5ea0ef68 has null extraction status and an empty dossier, so its geometry, role, and contact interfaces could not be characterized. Its single contact with the rest of the assembly is inferred only from the overall contact count. The assembly's connection strategy (adhesive bonding, press-fit nesting, snap engagement, or other) cannot be determined from the geometric evidence alone, since no fastener features or mechanical interlocks are present.
